title: Delete globalTaskStore.ts — the entire 399-line module is dead
description: globalTaskStore.ts (399 lines: file-backed JSON task store with add/update/delete/restore, getStats analytics, getTimeSeries charts) has exactly one reference in the whole repo: `import { globalTaskStore } from '../services/globalTaskStore.js'` at api/src/routes/agents.ts:3 — and `globalTaskStore.` is never used anywhere in that file (verified via grep: only the import line matches). The system moved to the DB-backed tasks store (api/src/services/database/tasks.ts); this whole module, its `data/global-todos.json` persistence, and its 170 lines of stats/time-series math are vestigial. Anyone reading it wastes real time assuming it is live (it even has careful atomic-write and corrupt-file-backup logic).
sketch: 1) Remove the unused import at api/src/routes/agents.ts:3. 2) Delete api/src/services/globalTaskStore.ts. 3) Run api tsc + tests. Optionally note in a commit message that data/global-todos.json is orphaned runtime data.
impact: ~400 lines removed, 2 files touched; eliminates an entire phantom subsystem from the mental model
guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Two small additions: (1) the strongest safety argument — worth stating in the commit message — is that tsc already elides the unused import, so dist/routes/agents.js has no reference to globalTaskStore and the module never loads at runtime; the deletion cannot change behavior even in error paths. (2) Stale build artifacts (api/dist/services/globalTaskStore.js/.d.ts/.map) will disappear on the next clean build; no manual dist cleanup needed. The orphaned data/global-todos.json runtime file is only ever touched if the module loads, which it does not — leave it alone or mention it in the commit message as the reviewer suggested.

title: users.ts: collapse the Google/Microsoft/GitHub copy-paste triple into provider-parameterized helpers
description: api/src/services/database/users.ts:100-236 is the same three functions pasted three times: getUserByGoogleId/createGoogleUser/linkGoogleId (100-144), getUserByMicrosoftId/createMicrosoftUser/linkMicrosoftId (146-190), getUserByGitHubId/createGitHubUser/linkGitHubId (192-236). The only difference between the triples is the column name (google_id / microsoft_id / github_id). Any fix (e.g. the 23505 handling, avatar COALESCE) must be applied 3 times today.
impact: ~90 lines removed in users.ts (137 → ~50); provider logic exists once
guidance: Sketch is correct; respect these traps: (1) Prefer the wrapper option (keep the 9 old export names as one-line delegations) — both test files stub the barrel via node:test mock.module('../database.js', { namedExports }) which replaces the ENTIRE export surface; if auth.ts is switched to import getUserByProviderId/createProviderUser/linkProviderId, both test files' namedExports blocks MUST gain those names in the same change or auth.ts import fails at test time (authorization.test.ts:31-57, workflow-pipeline.test.ts:~80-95). (2) Preserve exact console.error strings by deriving them from the column name ('Failed to get user by google_id:', 'Failed to link github_id:') — derive as `Failed to get user by ${col}` / `Failed to link ${col}`. (3) Do not name the create param 'email': auth.ts:676 passes a synthesized username (`login@users.noreply.github.com`) for GitHub, and the value lands in users.username — name it 'username'. (4) Preserve the per-function error asymmetry exactly: getUserByProviderId and linkProviderId catch → return null; createProviderUser maps 23505 to 'Username already exists' and rethrows everything else (no console.error in create). (5) The provider column must also be interpolated into the RETURNING lists of create (`RETURNING id, username, role, display_name, ${col}, avatar_url, created_at, updated_at`) and link (`RETURNING id, username, role, display_name, ${col}, avatar_url`) — they differ between create and link; don't unify them. (6) Keep linkProviderId's parameter binding order ($1=userId, $2=externalId, $3=avatarUrl) as in users.ts:134-137.

title: Remove unused exports from taskStateMachine.ts and agentSelector.ts
description: Grep over api/src (including tests) shows these exports have zero callers outside their own definition + the index.ts re-export: isTerminalStatus (taskStateMachine.ts:52-54, and with it the TERMINAL_STATUSES set at line 14), getFirstColumn (67-69), getOnEnterTransitions (159-164), getConditionalTransitions (169-178) — both subsumed by getMatchingTransitions which is what workflowEngine actually uses — and isAgentBusy (agentSelector.ts:106-109). They are advertised as public API in index.ts (lines 13, 16, 19-20, 36), so every reader assumes external consumers exist and traces them in vain.
sketch: Delete the five functions plus the TERMINAL_STATUSES set, and drop the corresponding lines from the export blocks in api/src/services/workflow/index.ts. tsc will confirm nothing references them.
impact: ~55 lines removed across 3 files; the state-machine public surface matches what is actually consumed
guidance: Sketch is correct as written. Traps to respect: (1) keep INACTIVE_STATUSES (taskStateMachine.ts:15) — only TERMINAL_STATUSES is dead; (2) keep Trigger — Trigger.CONDITION is still used by getWorkflowManagedStatuses (line 201) and workflowEngine, even though Trigger.ON_ENTER may become locally unused after deleting getOnEnterTransitions; (3) do NOT touch the similar-looking refreshLock/touchAgentBusy/hasLockForTask in agentSelector.ts — they ARE consumed by actionExecutor.ts:13 and workflowEngine.ts:32; (4) the _getFirstColumnStatus in agentManager/tasks.ts:360 is an unrelated independent implementation, not a caller — leave it alone. Run api tsc + the 244 tests to confirm.

title: One hasIdleAgentWithRole helper instead of three pasted predicates + a dead stub branch
description: The predicate `[...agents.values()].some(a => a.status === 'idle' && a.enabled !== false && (!role || a.role === role))` is copy-pasted three times: workflowEngine.ts:142-144, workflowEngine.ts:323-325, and agentManager/workflow.ts:28-30 (inside _evaluateCondition's own special-case for idle_agent_available). Meanwhile evaluateCondition in taskStateMachine.ts:116-121 contains a stub case for the same field that hardcodes fieldValue='false' with the comment "will be overridden by caller if needed" — unreachable through evaluateAllConditions (which intercepts the field at lines 144-147) and misleading for direct callers.
impact: 3 copies → 1; removes a misleading dead branch; ~15 lines net
guidance: Three corrections to the sketch: (1) Prefer placing the helper in agentSelector.ts, not taskStateMachine.ts — evaluateAllConditions already has a PARAMETER named hasIdleAgentWithRole (taskStateMachine.ts:140, a (role)=>boolean callback) and a module-level export with the same name but different arity (agents, role) would shadow confusingly inside that function. If it must live in taskStateMachine.ts, rename one of them. (2) In agentManager/workflow.ts:24-36, replace ONLY the .some() scan (lines 28-30); keep the neq inversion (line 31) and the console.log on true (line 32) — folding the whole special case into evaluateAllConditions would change logging. (3) When deleting the stub case at taskStateMachine.ts:116-121, note the field then falls through to default fieldValue='' instead of 'false' — behavior-identical for every existing caller (all intercept before reaching it) but flips the result for a hypothetical direct call with operator eq and value 'false'; leave a one-line comment in the switch pointing at evaluateAllConditions so future readers know the field is handled upstream. Also update the JSDoc @param at line 137 if the callback name changes.

title: Extract recordReassign() and saveThenEmitTaskUpdated() — the same 12-line ritual appears 5x
description: Two rituals are pasted across both workflow files. (a) The reassign history push `{status, at, by: 'workflow', type: 'reassign', assignee}` (with its `if (!history) history = []` guard) appears 4x: actionExecutor.ts:229-236 (executeAssignAgent), 264-271 (executeAssignAgentIndividual), 406-415 (executeRunAgent), workflowEngine.ts:561-568 (_autoAssignByColumn). (b) The deferred-emit dance `const payload = {...actualTask, agentId}; Promise.resolve(saveTaskToDb(...)).catch(() => {}).then(() => _emitTaskUpdated(...))` appears 5x: actionExecutor.ts:238-242, 272-277, 421-425, 476-480 and workflowEngine.ts:571-578 — and the workflowEngine copy re-implements _emitTaskUpdated's assigneeName/assigneeIcon enrichment by hand (572-574 duplicating actionExecutor.ts:61-68). The crucial save-before-emit ordering rationale is re-commented at each site.
impact: ~50 lines removed; emit-after-save invariant and assignee enrichment live in exactly one place
guidance: 1) BEHAVIOR DELTA TO ACKNOWLEDGE (the description understates this): _emitTaskUpdated also stamps task.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString() (actionExecutor.ts:60), which workflowEngine.ts:571-578 currently does NOT. Using the shared helper there adds the stamp — a deliberate consistency fix (without it the auto-assign emit can lose to stale loadTasks() merges, per the comment at actionExecutor.ts:58-60), but say so in the commit; if strict preservation is demanded, leave the workflowEngine emit as-is and only dedup the history push there. 2) Use `if (!task.history) task.history = []` in recordReassign, not `task.history ??= []` — the sketch's ??= differs for falsy non-nullish values; match the original guard exactly for free. 3) The sketch passes ONE object to both saveTaskToDb and _emitTaskUpdated; the originals make two separate spreads. saveTaskToDb defers serialization through a per-task write queue (api/src/services/database/tasks.ts:106-128), and _emitTaskUpdated mutates the payload (updatedAt, assigneeName/Icon) — the mutation lands after the save settles so it happens to be safe, but keep two spreads like the originals to stay obviously equivalent. 4) Scope guard: do NOT convert actionExecutor.ts:605-607 (emit WITHOUT save is deliberate — the comment explains the race it avoids) nor the catch path at actionExecutor.ts:566-567 (awaited save + raw _emit, different contract), nor the agentManager/tasks.ts copies (153-159, 238-248, 528 — raw this._emit, no enrichment, one is by:'user'). 5) recordReassign reads task.status at call time — call it at exactly the position the push occupied (all 4 sites push before any status mutation, so this is order-preserving).

title: actionExecutor: merge _runTitleMode/_runSetTypeMode and extract the streaming sendMessage wrapper
description: (a) _runTitleMode (613-639) and _runSetTypeMode (641-667) are byte-for-byte identical except for the prompt builder and the 2-line response post-processing — same maxLen slice, same try/catch with the same isUserStopError rethrow comment, same _saveExecutionLog success/failure pair, same finally agentUpdated. (b) The streaming wrapper — streamStart, accumulate fullResponse, streamChunk+thinking per chunk, workflowMeta, streamEnd+agentUpdated in finally — is pasted three times: _runRefineMode 675-700, _runDecideMode 757-776, _runExecuteMode 857-870/906-909.
impact: ~80 lines removed from a 912-line file; the five mode handlers become visibly distinct only in what is actually distinct
guidance:
Part (a) is good as sketched, with two constraints: (1) keep apply() inside the shared try — non-stop errors thrown by updateTaskTitle/updateTaskType must remain swallowed (best-effort, still returns { executed: true }), exactly as today; (2) keep the per-mode console messages ("title: generating" / "set_type: classifying", and the success logs) parameterized, not genericized. Note title's _saveExecutionLog(true) fires even when the parsed title is empty (no updateTaskTitle call) — the apply-table shape preserves this.

Part (b) MUST NOT use the sketched return-{result,fullResponse} shape. streamEnd/agentUpdated currently fire in a finally that wraps the per-mode post-processing, not just sendMessage: execute's try (858-909) encloses _saveExecutionLog, the completion checks, and the potentially minutes-long await _waitForExecutionComplete (888) plus its throw/early-return paths (885, 892-904); refine's try (676-700) encloses _saveExecutionLog + updateTaskText; decide's (758-776) encloses _saveExecutionLog. The sketch would emit streamEnd/agentUpdated before all of that — an observable wire-order change, and for execute a visible UI change (frontend STREAM_END at App.tsx:294-316 clears thinkingMap and arms the stream-buffer clear; today the live buffer/thinking persists through the wait loop, during which _waitForExecutionComplete emits nested stream:start/end pairs at tasks.ts:1026/1042-1043 whose final cleanup relies on the outer streamEnd firing last). Instead use a higher-order wrapper that takes the body as a callback so emit ordering is byte-identical: async function _withAgentStream(agentManager, agentId, body) { agentManager.wsEmitter.streamStart(agentId); try { return await body(); } finally { agentManager.wsEmitter.streamEnd(agentId); agentManager.wsEmitter.agentUpdated(agentId); } } — optionally plus a small chunk-collector factory for the fullResponse += chunk / streamChunk / thinking callback and the one-line workflowMeta. Each mode's entire existing try-body moves into body() unchanged.

Do not attempt to unify response extraction across modes: refine reads result?.content || fullResponse (690) while title/set_type treat the sendMessage return as a plain string (622, 650) — the return shape differs by path; leave each caller's extraction as-is.

title: actionExecutor: one CLI-terminal runner + one waitResult interpreter instead of three pasted copies
description: The CLI-runner branch — bindAgentRunner, sendTerminalInput({submit:true}), _waitForExecutionComplete({terminalDriven:true}), then the waitResult ladder ('error' → consume auth error + throw, 'stopped' → saveExecutionLog(false) + skipped, 'deleted' → skipped, success → saveExecutionLog(true)) — is duplicated between _runDecideMode (737-755) and _runExecuteMode (828-854). The same waitResult ladder appears a third time in the non-CLI execute wait at 888-904. Differences are tiny and easy to lose in the noise: execute throws on 'timeout' (843-845, 896-898) while decide has no timeout branch, and the error strings differ slightly ('Claude Code CLI...' vs 'Executor agent...').
impact: ~55 lines removed; the per-mode contract differences (timeout handling) become explicit parameters instead of diff-hunting
guidance: Corrections to the sketch: (1) The third copy (non-CLI execute, 888-904) must NOT call _saveExecutionLog(false) on 'stopped' — the log was already saved with success=true at line 872 before the wait. Add a flag (e.g. logOnStop: boolean) to _interpretWaitResult, or leave stopped-logging at the two CLI call sites; the sketch as written introduces a duplicate, contradictory execution-log entry on the non-CLI user-stop path. (2) The sketch's claim that error strings differ between decide and execute CLI branches is wrong — lines 746 and 841 use the identical 'Claude Code CLI ended in an authentication or runtime error' string; only the non-CLI copy (894) uses 'Executor agent ended in error before completing the task'. errorLabel still covers this, just pass the same label from both CLI sites. Behavior-preservation traps: (a) the decide handler must NOT return {executed:true} after the success log — it falls through to the decision-verification block at 780-811 (before/after status+textLen comparison and _decideNoDecisionCount logic); (b) decide's 'timeout' currently falls through to _saveExecutionLog(true,'decide') then the decided check — throwOnTimeout=false must return null so the caller still logs success; (c) in the non-CLI path the interpreter call must remain inside the existing try block so the finally (906-909, streamEnd + agentUpdated) still runs when it throws; (d) the success path for the non-CLI site logs nothing after the wait (already logged at 872) — the post-interpret _saveExecutionLog(true, mode) line applies only to the two CLI sites; (e) preserve the optional chaining on agentManager._consumeTaskAuthError?.(). Scope limit: do NOT fold in the fourth ladder at api/src/services/agentManager/tasks.ts:1262-1276 — it has a different contract (returns waitResult to its caller, computes log success as waitResult !== 'error' && !== 'timeout', calls _consumeTaskAuthError without ?.). The trivial console.log wording differences between the two CLI injection sites ('injecting prompt' vs 'injecting task prompt') should either stay at the call sites or be passed as a label.

title: Unify the completedActionIdx / _completedActionIdx twin fields into one
description: The chain-resume index lives under two names that are always written, cleared, and read as a pair: workflowEngine.ts:471-472 and 483-484 (`actualTask._completedActionIdx = i; actualTask.completedActionIdx = i;`), 525-527 (delete one, null the other), actionExecutor.ts:322-324, agentManager/tasks.ts:195-196, agentManager/conversation.ts:30-31, routes/tasks.ts:349-350 and 461-462. Every read needs the coalescing dance `task.completedActionIdx ?? task._completedActionIdx` (workflowEngine.ts:279, 417). The underscore twin is a leftover from when the field was in-memory-only; since rowToTask (database/tasks.ts:50) and saveTaskToDb both handle `completedActionIdx`, the second name only adds a 'why are there two?' question at six call sites.
sketch: Keep only `completedActionIdx` (the persisted name). Mechanical changes: (1) each paired write becomes `task.completedActionIdx = i;`; (2) each paired clear becomes `task.completedActionIdx = null;`; (3) reads drop the `?? task._completedActionIdx` fallback; (4) the startup re-arm at workflowEngine.ts:279 and the resume gate at 417 read the single field; (5) update workflow.test.ts §7 (lines 416-500, 800) which exercises both names. Resume-on-restart behavior is unchanged because the persisted column is already the source of truth after a reboot.
impact: ~25 lines net, 6 src files + 1 test file; eliminates the most confusing parallel structure in the workflow engine
guidance: One correction to the proposal's premise and one behavior note for the implementer. (1) The claim 'always written, cleared, and read as a pair' has exactly one exception: the recurrence reset at agentManager/tasks.ts:765 and 788 nulls ONLY the persisted name and never deletes memTask._completedActionIdx (nor memTask._pendingOnEnter in the mirror block at 782-797). Today, with the `??` coalescing reads, a lingering underscore value survives that reset (`null ?? 1` → 1), and if the lingering _pendingOnEnter matches the reset column, _executeActionChain (workflowEngine.ts:417-419) resumes mid-chain instead of starting at action 0. After unification the reset becomes fully effective — this is a behavior change in that edge case, but it fixes a latent bug consistent with the surrounding code's intent (lines 761-770 reset every other execution marker for a fresh cycle); state this explicitly in the commit message instead of trying to preserve the buggy coalescing. (2) Mechanical details: use `= null` (not delete) as the cleared value to match the existing persisted-clear convention — reads use `??` and `typeof === 'number'`, so null vs undefined is interchangeable, and saveTaskToDb (database/tasks.ts:177) normalizes both; remove the now-dead `delete task._completedActionIdx` lines at conversation.ts:30, routes/tasks.ts:349 and 461, actionExecutor.ts:322, agentManager/tasks.ts:195; collapse the dual typeof check at workflowEngine.ts:525 to the single field. (3) The workflow.test.ts §7 tests (416-500, 800) mostly re-implement the two-field dance inline rather than driving the engine, so rewriting them to one field is safe and trivial; do not weaken the one test (419) that asserts the clear happens before change_status.

title: workflowEngine: split 215-line recheckPendingTransitions into named phases and merge the two parallel retry maps
description: recheckPendingTransitions (187-401) does five unrelated jobs inline: evict stale condition locks (192-205), sweep retry bookkeeping (212-221), load board transitions (223-249), the one-shot post-restart re-arm (264-285), and the per-agent/per-task/per-transition triple loop (288-400) whose body itself branches into the on_enter-retry path vs the conditional-chain path. On top of that, retry state is two parallel Maps — agentManager._onEnterRetryTimestamps and agentManager._onEnterRetryCounts — keyed by the same string `${agentId}:${task.id}:lastRetry` (the ':lastRetry' suffix is vestigial), forced to be created (345-346), read/written (348-357), swept (212-221), and cleared (487-493) in lockstep.
impact: Function bodies become 10-40 lines each with self-describing names; ~20 lines net removed; retry state can no longer half-desync
guidance: Sketch is sound; respect these traps: (1) Keep _recheckTask SYNCHRONOUS — the original loop body has no awaits; an awaited async helper inserts microtask boundaries between task iterations, changing interleaving with the fire-and-forget processColumnEntry/_executeActionChain promises (the _processingTasks and _conditionProcessing locks are set synchronously and must stay so). (2) Keep _reArmInterruptedChainsOnce AFTER the empty-transMap early return, as the sketch shows — _startupReArmDone is a one-shot module flag and the original leaves it false when no board has relevant transitions (return at line 249 precedes the re-arm at 264), deferring re-arm to a later tick; hoisting it would consume the one-shot prematurely. (3) _loadRelevantBoardTransitions must preserve the error path of lines 223-229: catch the getAllBoardWorkflows failure, emit the same console.error, and cause the caller to return (returning an empty transMap achieves the identical early-return). (4) When the per-transition `break`s become returns inside _recheckTask, the preceding lock cleanups (`agentManager._conditionProcessing.delete(lockKey)` at 340, 353, 374) must be kept — and a break must end ALL remaining matching transitions for that task, not fall through to the next one. (5) Map-merge key parity: line 347 builds the key from the loop `agentId` while 489/492 use `task.agentId`; these are equal because both chain entry points carry agentId on the task ({ ...task, agentId } spreads at 362 and 384), so keep each site's existing identifier when dropping ':lastRetry'. (6) Preserve the missing-entry defaults: first attempt must read count=0 / lastRetry=0 (lines 348, 351 use `|| 0`), i.e., absent merged entry behaves as { ts: 0, count: 0 }. (7) Keep the lazy-init + existence-guard pattern for the merged map (the sweep at 212 guards on map existence); optionally declare _onEnterRetry on the AgentManager type at api/src/services/agentManager/index.ts:174 next to _conditionProcessing for discoverability — the current maps are undeclared lazy properties.

title: actionExecutor: decompose the 260-line executeRunAgent into phase helpers
description: executeRunAgent (347-609) handles, in one body: lock acquisition (353-358), agent selection (362-375), the heartbeat timer (381-390), actionRunning flag setup + reassign + save/emit (400-426), the repo auto-switch with its own 30-line inline error path (428-493), the 5-way mode dispatch (498-518), user-stop vs real-error catch handling (521-575), and a 30-line finally cleanup (576-608). The repo-switch catch block (457-491) is the worst offender: it hand-rolls error history + actionRunning cleanup + error emit that closely mirror what markTaskError and the outer chain handler already provide, and one must read 65 lines to learn 'switch the agent to the task repo, fail the action if that fails'.
impact: executeRunAgent shrinks to ~110 lines of visible phase structure; the repo-switch contract becomes testable in isolation
guidance: 1) Do NOT take the description's 'closely mirrors markTaskError' literally. The switch-error block (462-481) is deliberately DIFFERENT from markTaskError (taskErrors.ts:57-110): it leaves the task in its current column (no status change, no errorFromStatus), pushes a history entry with status=currentStatus (markTaskError pushes status='error', from=prevStatus), and skips _saveExecutionLog. Extract the block as-is inside _ensureAgentOnTaskRepo; substituting markTaskError would move the task to the error column on switch failure — a behavior change. Same for the error-report payload: the switch path uses project: task.project (485) while the outer catch uses agent.project || task.project (547) — keep both as-is. 2) Fix the sketched signatures: _ensureAgentOnTaskRepo also needs mode (historyEntry.actionMode at 474) and task.agentId (save payload + _emitTaskUpdated use agentId: task.agentId, which actualTask does not carry); _markActionRunning likewise needs task.agentId. Pass them via ctx. 3) _startLockHeartbeat must keep the heartbeatStartedAt capture, the LOCK_HEARTBEAT_MAX_MS self-clear, and the (heartbeat as any).unref?.() call, and return the interval so the finally can clearInterval it. 4) Keep the user-stop/error catch (521-575) and the finally (576-608) inline in executeRunAgent — they close over execStartMsgIdx, execStartedAt, actualTask, agent, mode, heartbeat; extracting them is not in scope and would balloon parameter lists. 5) The 'saveThenEmit from the dedup proposal' reference is optional — the extraction works standalone with the existing fire-and-forget Promise chains copied verbatim (preserve the .catch(() => {}).then(emit) order: emit happens even when save fails). 6) No tests cover this path (workflow tests only hand-simulate actionRunning flags), so verify with tsc + full api suite and a careful diff read; consider adding a unit test for _ensureAgentOnTaskRepo's failure contract as part of the change.

title: tasks.ts: queryTasks() helper for the 11 identical SELECT wrappers + one field→column map in updateTaskFields
description: Eleven functions in api/src/services/database/tasks.ts repeat the exact same shell — getPool guard, pool.query(`${TASK_SELECT} WHERE ... `), rows.map(rowToTask), catch → console.error + fallback: getTasksByAgent (62-75), getAllTasks (77-87), getTaskById (89-100), getDeletedTasks (238-248), getDeletedTaskById (250-261), getActiveTasksByAgent (425-438), getTasksByBoard (443-456), getTasksByAssignee (487-500), getActiveTaskForExecutor (506-524), getRecurringTasks (588-602), getTasksByStatusAndBoard (720-746). Only the WHERE/ORDER clause and params differ. Separately, updateTaskFields (751-800) maintains two parallel structures that must be kept in sync: the `allowed` snake_case column list (754-762) and the `camelToSnake` rename map (764-772) — a field is writable only if its mapped name happens to appear in the other list.
impact: ~130 lines removed from an 800-line file; adding a task query becomes a one-liner; updateTaskFields allow-list can no longer drift
guidance: Four corrections to make it strictly behavior-preserving: (1) Log prefixes vary ('Failed to load tasks for agent' vs 'Failed to get task' vs 'Failed to get deleted tasks'...); the sketch's `Failed to load ${label}` template would change ~6 of 11 messages — pass the full message prefix per getter instead of interpolating into a fixed template. (2) In updateTaskFields, the sketch's `if (!col) continue` is weaker than the current `allowed.includes(col)` guard: a fields key like 'toString' resolves via prototype chain to a truthy function, which current code skips (not in allowed) but the sketch would inject into SQL, failing the whole UPDATE instead of skipping the key. Use Object.hasOwn(COLUMN_BY_FIELD, key) (or a null-prototype object) for the lookup, and precompute `const COLUMNS = new Set(Object.values(COLUMN_BY_FIELD))` at module level for the snake_case passthrough — which must be kept, since current code accepts already-snake_case keys like 'board_id'. (3) getTaskById/getDeletedTaskById currently map only rows[0]; queryOneTask mapping all rows then taking [0] is result-identical (id is PK) — fine, but don't add LIMIT 1 to the SQL (that would change the query text for no behavioral reason). (4) getTasksByStatusAndBoard builds its conditions inside the existing try; building (array pushes/template strings) cannot throw, so moving it before the helper call is safe. Also preserve the exact `[]`-on-no-pool / null-on-no-pool fallbacks (queryOneTask returning (await queryTasks(...))[0] ?? null already does). Prefer keeping `export async function` declarations over converting to arrow consts — smaller diff, same effect.

title: One createJsonDocStore() factory for the four identical JSONB-document CRUD modules
description: Four modules implement the same CRUD on an (id, data JSONB, created_at, updated_at) table: skills.ts (getAllSkills/saveSkill/deleteSkillFromDb, 41 lines), mcpServers.ts (same + encryptFields(['apiKey']), 45 lines), llmConfigs.ts (same + getById + decryptFields, 55 lines), agentSkills.ts (getAll/getById/save/delete at 3-13 and 50-87; searchAgentSkills is genuinely unique). The upsert SQL `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET data = $2, updated_at = NOW()`, the getPool guards, and the catch/log shapes are copy-pasted 12+ times with only the table name, ORDER BY, and secret-field list varying.
impact: ~120 lines removed across 4 files; one place to fix upsert/error behavior; existing export names preserved so the database.ts barrel and callers are untouched
guidance: Four traps to respect for strict behavior preservation: (1) Log labels differ per entity and operation ('Failed to load skills:' vs 'Failed to load MCP servers:' vs 'Failed to get LLM config:'). The sketch omits this — add a label option, e.g. createJsonDocStore('mcp_servers', { label: 'MCP server', labelPlural: 'MCP servers' }), and emit 'Failed to load ${labelPlural}:', 'Failed to save ${label}:', etc., logging err.message (not err) as today. No test asserts these, but the proposal claims behavior preservation, so keep them byte-identical. (2) Delete return unification: remove() returning rowCount>0 changes skills/mcpServers/llmConfigs deletes from undefined to boolean. Verified safe — all 8 call sites discard the return — but make it deliberate, not accidental; do not start branching on it in callers as part of this refactor. (3) agentSkills must pass orderBy: 'updated_at DESC' (others default 'created_at'); getting this wrong silently reorders UI lists. (4) The sketch detaches methods (export const getAllLlmConfigs = store.getAll), so the factory must use closures over (table, opts), never `this`. Minor details that already line up: unify getById as `const d = rows[0]?.data; return d ? dec(d) : null` — matches both llmConfigs (ternary) and agentSkills (|| null) since both coerce falsy to null; crypto.ts encryptFields/decryptFields already accept readonly string[]; skills/mcpServers simply don't export the store's unused getById. Keep searchAgentSkills (agentSkills.ts:15-48) and all of agents.ts out, as sketched.
